heading: 1. Project Overview
content: This document proposes how three already-surveyed systems, OpenWiki, OB1, and reasoning-bank, combine into the Local Knowledge Synthesis backend that Omni Claw's C++ engine queries mid-inference before compiling a meta-prompt. Omni Claw's blueprint describes this step as pulling skill blocks, API schemas, and historical code contexts out of local Obsidian or Markor directories, a placeholder rather than a real queryable memory system. This architecture replaces that placeholder with three components: OpenWiki supplies the maintenance discipline (the SKILL.md convention keeping the knowledge base readable and self-describing); OB1 (Open Brain) supplies the actual memory store (a thoughts table with HNSW cosine retrieval and a remote-only MCP server); reasoning-bank supplies the retrieval strategy (distilled natural-language memory items from both successful and failed trajectories, retrieved by embedding similarity, capped small). The three map onto three separate concerns: how the knowledge base stays maintainable (OpenWiki), where knowledge lives and how it's queried (OB1), and what gets pulled into context for a task and why (reasoning-bank) -- kept conceptually separate to match how the Dual-Agent Talker-Reasoner pattern already separates retrieval (Query/Talker agent) from execution (Executor agent).

heading: 2. Architecture Map
content: Five logical layers, none currently existing as code, describing the target shape. Layer 5, Meta-Prompt Compiler (Omni Claw C++ engine), receives a distilled context payload, compiles the final meta-prompt, streams it to the AI Chat Box for human review before dispatch. Layer 4, Retrieval Strategy (reasoning-bank pattern), embeds the current query, cosine-matches against stored memory items, caps retrieval count, distinguishes success-pattern items from failure-pattern items. Layer 3, Memory Store and Query Interface (OB1), is the thoughts table (Supabase/pgvector), HNSW index, SHA-256 dedup on ingest, with a remote-only MCP server as the only access path, never local/stdio, a hard rule. Layer 2, Maintenance and Structure (OpenWiki convention), is SKILL.md per capability area, with a doc-generation agent keeping the wiki self-describing; taxonomy is skills/ (behavior), recipes/ plus schemas/ plus thoughts (memory), extensions/ plus primitives/ (curated reference). Layer 1, Ingestion Pipeline (this project's standing rules), produces clean Markdown plus JSONL chunks plus deduped URL lists per doc, with Gemini-sourced content code-repaired and flagged. The stack is model-agnostic at Layers 1-4: the ingestion pipeline, OB1 store, and reasoning-bank retrieval pattern don't care whether Layer 5 is Omni Claw's on-device Gemma-4 12B, AESOP's cloud Claude API, or the home-hub dual-agent executor from the Final Memory Layer/P2P doc -- the same backend serves all three deployment profiles.

heading: 3. Data and Memory Flow (Ingestion)
content: How raw material becomes queryable memory: (1) a raw source arrives (PDF, doc, transcript, code repo) and is never modified in place, per this project's standing pipeline rules; (2) Layer 1 ingestion produces a clean Markdown reconstruction plus JSONL chunking (one object per logical section with id, section, heading, content, source_urls, tags) plus deduped URL extraction, with Gemini-sourced content code-repaired and flagged; (3) Layer 2 categorization has the doc-generation agent decide which OB1 taxonomy bucket the content belongs in, skills/ (a reusable behavior pack gets a SKILL.md), recipes/ or schemas/ (structured reference data), or raw thoughts (freeform memory items destined for the vector store); (4) Layer 3 storage lands content in OB1's thoughts table, embedded for HNSW cosine retrieval and SHA-256 fingerprinted for dedup, a write-time step not something the executor does per-query; (5) Layer 4 distillation is ongoing, not one-time: as Omni Claw runs tasks, the reasoning-bank pattern extracts new memory items post-hoc from both successful and failed task trajectories, writing them back into OB1 as new thoughts, so the memory store grows from the AI's own operating history, not just human-fed documents. Retrieval and distillation are two directions through the same store, not a one-way pipe.

heading: 4. Runtime Query Flow (The Live Path)
content: What happens when the user triggers a meta-prompt compilation, mapping onto Omni Claw's existing Meta-Prompt Verification Loop: (1) user taps the internal mic in the AI Chat Floating Box, speaks a rough intent; (2) Silero VAD isolates speech, raw text/audio routes over local loopback to the C++ engine, unchanged from the existing blueprint; (3) new step -- instead of reading Obsidian files, the engine embeds the transcribed intent and sends a retrieval request to OB1's MCP server, remote MCP only per OB1's hard rule, a real network round-trip even for an on-device engine; (4) OB1 runs HNSW cosine similarity search against the thoughts table, returns a ranked list; (5) the reasoning-bank retrieval strategy caps this to n=1-10 items, returned as the distilled payload, small enough the executor's context doesn't bloat, matching the Dual-Agent Talker-Reasoner pattern's core complaint that uncapped retrieval defeats the purpose; (6) the C++ engine synthesizes the meta-prompt using this payload plus the raw intent, streams it into the AI Chat Box as editable text, the human-in-the-loop step unchanged; (7) on dispatch (local tool call, cloud API, or CLI sub-agent spin-up), the task executes, and its outcome (success or failure) becomes a candidate for the next Layer 4 distillation pass.

heading: 5. Deployment Profiles
content: Three profiles corresponding to the three device topologies documented across this project, playing the role AESOP's device profiles play but for knowledge-backend deployment scale rather than voice/TTS backend selection. Phone-Only matches AESOP's cloud-delegated mode or Omni Claw running standalone without a home network: OB1 hosted remotely (cloud Supabase project) since remote-MCP-only is a hard rule anyway, retrieval happens over the phone's normal internet connection, the simplest profile, works away from home, latency depends entirely on cloud round-trip. Home-Hub matches the Final Memory Layer/P2P doc's 3-device mesh (Razr Ultra plus Jetson Nano plus Rubik Pi 3): OB1's Postgres/Supabase instance runs on the Jetson Nano's attached SSD, phone reaches it via Tailscale P2P when home, matching that doc's governed continuity memory layer exactly, since OB1 IS that layer formalized. Full Mesh is the Home-Hub profile plus the Rubik Pi 3 Red Agent Auditor: same OB1 placement, but retrieval payloads for code-generation tasks also get audited by the Rubik Pi's Gemma-4 E2B gatekeeper before execution, adding the verification step the Final Memory Layer doc specifies for anything the executor generates and is about to run. None of these profiles requires a different memory architecture, only where OB1's database physically runs and how the phone reaches it changes -- a deliberate design property so the backend doesn't need rebuilding when the user moves from phone-only to phone-plus-home-mesh.

heading: 6. Component Reference
content: OpenWiki (doc-generation CLI agent, LangGraph/DeepAgents-based) has no memory/RAG/MCP of its own; it's the authoring/maintenance tool, not the runtime store. Its contribution is the SKILL.md convention, confined to Layer 2, deciding how new content gets organized, not runtime retrieval. OB1 (Open Brain) is the mature memory backend: thoughts table core schema, HNSW cosine similarity retrieval, SHA-256 fingerprint ingestion dedup, and a remote-only MCP server by hard rule (no claude_desktop_config.json, no StdioServerTransport, no local Node.js server; deploys as a Supabase Edge Function). This is a genuine architectural constraint: the C++ engine's retrieval call is a real network request even when running fully on-device, since OB1 will not run as an in-process/local server. Taxonomy is skills/ (behavior packs), recipes/ plus schemas/ plus thoughts (memory), extensions/ plus primitives/ (curated project reference), mapping onto the skill/memory/project-reference buckets this ingestion project has been sorting material into. reasoning-bank is not an app but a research pattern (Google, ICLR 2026 submission) for distilled natural-language memory items (Title/Description/Content) from agent trajectories, retrieved by embedding cosine similarity, capped small. Critically, memory items come from both success and failure trajectories -- a failed task becomes a retrievable what-didn't-work item, exactly what a Rubik Pi Red Agent Auditor rejection should feed back into. The Dual-Agent Talker-Reasoner pattern (from the Final Memory Layer/P2P doc, a named architecture pattern not a separate repo): the Query/Talker Agent's job (search, isolate a slice, package a micro-payload) is precisely Layers 3-4 of this document; the Executor Agent's job (receive the payload, do the task, wipe its own history) is Layer 5.

heading: 7. Retrieval and Model Strategy Guide
content: HNSW cosine similarity (OB1's native mechanism) is the retrieval baseline; reasoning-bank's contribution caps how many results get used (n=1-10), not the similarity mechanism itself. Prompt caching interaction: the retrieved memory-item payload should be treated as the cache-eligible static prefix whenever the downstream model is Claude-API-class (the AESOP cloud-delegation path, or a CLI sub-agent calling Claude); since a user's working session tends to stay on-topic, placing distilled context early in the prompt with a cache_control breakpoint captures the 90-percent-discount cache-read rate. This is a direct, mechanical link between the memory-retrieval architecture and the prompt-caching research already condensed and flagged high priority. Sub-agent fan-out: if a task spins up multiple CLI sub-agents (matching the Final Memory Layer/P2P doc's Cache Pre-warming pattern), they should all read the same retrieved payload rather than each independently querying OB1, both to avoid the concurrency trap (concurrent cold cache writes) and to guarantee sub-agents don't silently diverge on context. On-device vs cloud retrieval cost: retrieval itself is cheap and network-bound, not compute-bound, and doesn't touch the on-device NPU; the NPU/compute-cost concerns from the NPU test-time-scaling paper apply to Layer 5 (generation), not Layers 3-4 of this architecture.

heading: 8. Interaction Patterns
content: The triggers this backend needs to serve: meta-prompt compilation (the primary case, triggered by the internal mic in the AI Chat Box); skill invocation (when a compiled meta-prompt resolves to a known capability, the system should retrieve the matching SKILL.md rather than re-deriving something the wiki already documents); post-task distillation (not user-triggered, happens automatically after any dispatched task completes or fails); cross-device continuity queries (the Home-Hub and Full Mesh profiles imply a task started on the phone away from home needing its prior context once back on the mesh, a straightforward OB1 retrieval since the store doesn't care which device asks, but the routing decision of whether to reach OB1 remotely right now or wait for Tailscale is an open question).

heading: 9. Extending the System / Adding Skills
content: Adding a new skill: write a SKILL.md (OpenWiki convention), let the Layer 2 doc-generation agent ingest it into OB1's skills/ bucket, with no code changes needed to Layers 3-5, the entire point of keeping maintenance separate from storage. Adding a new memory source: run it through the standing Layer 1 ingestion pipeline (clean md plus JSONL plus URLs) exactly as this project has been doing for every PDF/doc processed so far. Adjusting retrieval behavior: the n=1-10 cap and success/failure distinction are reasoning-bank-pattern parameters, not OB1 schema changes, tunable without touching the memory store. Adding a new deployment profile (e.g. a future desktop/laptop version, flagged as an open question in the Omni Claw blueprint's own open-questions section): per the deployment profiles section, this should only require deciding where OB1's database runs and how the new device reaches it, not redesigning Layers 3-4.

heading: 10. Known Gaps and Open Questions
content: The MCP-between-CLI-and-Kotlin-app question, raised directly by the user in the NotebookLM prompt-caching conversation, is still open: this document assumes OB1's remote MCP server is reachable from the C++ engine, but whether the Kotlin app itself should also expose an MCP server for device-control purposes (client/server roles reversed) is a separate unresolved architectural question flagged as needing independent research. The third repo the user couldn't name, mentioned in the Final Memory Layer/P2P doc as in between OB1 and Open-Wiki, remains unidentified and may fill a gap this document doesn't cover, or may overlap with reasoning-bank's role. Retrieval routing when off-mesh: whether/how the system should retry, queue, or gracefully degrade when the phone can't currently reach OB1 isn't specified here. Auditor-to-memory feedback loop mechanics: this document asserts Rubik Pi Auditor rejections should become failure-pattern memory items, but the actual write path (Auditor writes directly to OB1, or rejection routes back through the Jetson executor first) isn't specified, deliberately left for a future implementation-level doc. Cost of remote-only MCP for latency-sensitive on-device interactions: OB1's hard rule against local/stdio MCP is good for the security model but means every retrieval is a network round trip, and whether this is acceptable for Omni Claw's presumably-fast meta-prompt compilation loop hasn't been measured against real latency numbers.

heading: 11. Source and Provenance Log
content: Every claim in this document traces to material already condensed in the Claude_master_wiki Drive folder: the Local Knowledge Synthesis concept traces to the omni-claw-blueprint doc; architecture, data flow, and query flow trace to the openwiki, ob1, and reasoning-bank component surveys plus the omni-claw-blueprint's runtime trigger description; deployment profiles trace to the aesop-full-reference device-profile pattern and the final-memory-layer-p2p-pipeline hardware topology; component details trace to the openwiki, ob1, and reasoning-bank docs plus the final-memory-layer-p2p-pipeline's Dual-Agent Talker-Reasoner description; retrieval and caching strategy traces to the notebooklm-prompt-caching-planning-qa and anthropic-prompt-caching-docs, plus the final-memory-layer-p2p-pipeline's sub-agent caching section and the scaling-llm-test-time-compute-npu paper's compute-cost boundary; interaction patterns trace to the omni-claw-blueprint's meta-prompt loop and the final-memory-layer-p2p-pipeline's task workflow phases; the extension pattern traces to the llm-wiki-termux-setup skill package as a worked example; open questions trace to the notebooklm-prompt-caching-planning-qa's MCP question and the final-memory-layer-p2p-pipeline's unnamed third repo; and the 11-section format template itself is reused from aesop-full-reference per explicit instruction. No new external URLs are introduced by this document; it is a synthesis of already-ingested sources, not a new raw-source ingestion.
